en.extract:
The black-bellied firefinch (Lagonosticta rara) is a common species of estrildid finch found in Africa.
It is native to the Guinean forest–savanna mosaic, the southern Sahel and northern Sudan (region). It has an estimated global extent of occurrence of 2,300,000 km2.
The status of the species is evaluated as Least Concern.


References

BirdLife Species Factsheet

The '''black-bellied firefinch''' ('''''Lagonosticta rara''''') is a common species of [[estrildid finch]] found in Africa.

It is native to the [[Guinean forest–savanna mosaic]], the southern [[Sahel]] and northern [[Sudan (region)]]. It has an estimated global extent of occurrence of 2,300,000 km<sup>2</sup>.

The status of the species is evaluated as [[Least Concern]].

zh.extract:
黑腹火雀（学名：Lagonosticta rara）是梅花雀科火雀属的一种，为非洲的常见物种。其全球活动范围有2,300,000平方千米。分布于贝宁、布基纳法索、喀麦隆、中非共和国、乍得、刚果民主共和国、科特迪瓦、加纳、几内亚、肯尼亚、利比里亚、尼日利亚、塞内加尔、塞拉利昂、苏丹、多哥和乌干达。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。


参考文献
BirdLife International. Lagonosticta rara. The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species 2009.   [2010-11-04] （英语）.
